solution:
def baseNeg2(self, n: int) -> str:
    result = ""
    while n > 0:
        result = str(n % 2) + result
        n //= 2
    return result[::-1]

efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(log(n))
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW

Explanation:
The algorithm uses a while loop to convert the integer n to its base -2 representation. It calculates the remainder of n divided by 2 in each iteration and appends it to the result string. Then, it updates n by dividing it by 2. Finally, it reverses the result string using slicing (result[::-1]). This solution has a time complexity of O(log(n)) due to the division operation in each iteration, and a space complexity of O(1) as it only uses a constant amount of space for the result string.
